title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — July 2, 2026
headline: El Salvador's first formal assessment proposes a Developing-to-Critical band crossing at 15.0 of 100.
summary: El Salvador received its first formal benchmark assessment on July 2, 2026. The assessment generates a proposed score of 15.0 of 100 — the Critical band — against a seed score of 20.3 of 100 that was never formally evaluated. The proposed delta is -5.3, a Developing-to-Critical band crossing. The evidence includes the July 2025 indefinite re-election constitutional amendment (approved 57-3), the May 2025 foreign-agents law, the forced exile of Cristosal — El Salvador's leading human rights organization — and a three-year state of exception with approximately 87,000 people detained without due process. The Action dimension score is held at 2.4 to credit genuine homicide reduction. The published El Salvador score remains 20.3 of 100 while the proposal is under review. This is the first change proposal generated in eleven cycles, ending a ten-cycle run with an empty queue. Fourteen additional entities were confirmed at their published scores. Taiwan received its first formal baseline at 83.0 of 100 in the Exemplary band — rank 9 in the Countries Index. Russia's July 1-2 Kyiv mass strike — 27-plus killed, 70-plus missiles, approximately 500 drones — is scored against Russia; Ukraine is confirmed at 50.0 of 100. Venezuela's earthquake death toll rose to 2,595 on Day 8; the July 8 assessment checkpoint is six days away. Five structured subdimension evidence records were written tonight — the first cycle to produce per-subdimension evidence at scale. Thirteen false-positive screening records were documented.

topSignals.title: El Salvador 20.3 of 100 Published — First Formal Assessment Proposes 15.0 of 100 Critical Band; Developing-to-Critical Band Crossing Proposed; Published Score Unchanged Pending Review
topSignals.whyItMatters: El Salvador's seed score of 20.3 had never been formally evaluated. The first assessment proposes 15.0 of 100 — a Developing-to-Critical band crossing. The evidence: indefinite re-election amendment, foreign-agents law, Cristosal forced into exile, 87,000 detained without due process. The published score stays at 20.3 until the proposal is acted on.
topSignals.description: El Salvador holds a published score of 20.3 of 100 in the Developing band — a seed estimate from the original index build, never previously assessed with per-entity evidence. Tonight's first formal assessment proposes a score of 15.0 of 100 — a delta of -5.3, a crossing from Developing to Critical. The composite reconstruction: eight benchmark dimensions scored {Awareness 1.6, Empathy 1.6, Action 2.4, Equity 1.2, Boundaries 1.4, Accountability 1.2, Systems 1.8, Integrity 1.6}, producing a base score of 12.5 and an integration premium of zero (eight weak dimensions). Result: 15.0 of 100. The codified evidence record driving seven of eight dimensions into Critical-band anchors: the July 2025 constitutional amendment allowing indefinite presidential re-election, approved by the Legislative Assembly 57 votes to 3; the May 2025 foreign-agents law; the forced exile of Cristosal — El Salvador's leading human rights organization, active for 25 years — after its executive director stated 'We are forced to choose between prison or exile'; the arrest of anti-corruption chief Ruth Lopez; the forced exile of 140-plus human rights defenders and journalists; and a three-year state of exception with approximately 87,000 people detained without due process and custodial deaths documented by Cristosal and WOLA. The Action (ACT) dimension is held at 2.4 — above the published seed of 2.0 — to credit genuine homicide reduction under the Bukele administration. This credit distinguishes El Salvador from peers in the 9–13 range. The proposed score of 15.0 of 100 places El Salvador above Bahrain (9.4 of 100) and Mali (12.5 of 100) while below Venezuela (18.0 of 100) and China (19.5 of 100). The published score of 20.3 of 100 remains the operative score until a decision is made. Confidence: medium.

evidence.claim: Human Rights Watch September 2025: systematic dismantling of democratic checks and civic space documented.

evidence.quote: El Salvador's Legislative Assembly approved a constitutional amendment allowing indefinite presidential re-election with 57 votes in favor and three opposed
evidence.claim: Al Jazeera August 2025: indefinite re-election codified, dismantling term limits; term extended to 6 years; presidential runoff removed.

evidence.quote: We are forced to choose between prison or exile
evidence.claim: NPR July 2025: Cristosal, El Salvador's leading human rights organization after 25 years, forced out of the country under threat of prosecution.

evidence.quote: Four years of ongoing human rights violations in El Salvador and the erosion of democracy
evidence.claim: WOLA March 2026: sustained mass-detention and rights-erosion record documented across four years.

topSignals.title: Taiwan 83.0 of 100: First Formal Baseline — Exemplary Band, Rank 9; Universal Healthcare, Marriage Equality, Democratic Governance Confirmed
topSignals.whyItMatters: Taiwan had never been formally assessed in this benchmark. Its first score of 83.0 of 100 places it in the Exemplary band at rank 9 in the Countries Index. The assessment surfaced on Chinese military and legal pressure — but Taiwan's own conduct is what drives the score. Chinese coercion is scored against China, not Taiwan.
topSignals.description: Taiwan receives its first formal benchmark score of 83.0 of 100 — the Exemplary band, ranked 9th in the Countries Index. The composite reconstructs cleanly: a base score of 75.0 plus an integration premium of 8.0. The integration premium is awarded for consistent high performance across all eight benchmark dimensions with near-zero inter-dimension variance. Taiwan meets that bar on seven of eight dimensions — only Equity (EQU) scores at 3.5, one weak dimension, which zeros down the maximum premium slightly. Within-window evidence is consistent with 83.0: universal healthcare covers the full population; Taiwan became the first country in Asia to legalize same-sex marriage; the Economist Intelligence Unit rates Taiwan's democratic support at 91 percent; the Littoral Combat Command formation defends civilians, not projects offensive force. Taiwan surfaced in the scanner on the basis of Chinese naval exercises and legal-coercion pressure — including the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law's Article 63 diaspora-liability provision — but those are China's conduct, scored against China. Taiwan's own defensive posture is a civilian-protection measure, not a compassion harm and not a threshold-clearing positive swing above 83.0. Taiwan is confirmed at 83.0 of 100; confidence: medium.

evidence.claim: Taiwan universal healthcare: the National Health Insurance program covers approximately 99.9 percent of the population; the Economist Intelligence Unit's 2025 Democracy Index rates Taiwan at 8.92 of 10 — 'full democracy', ranked 10th globally; 91 percent of Taiwanese support the democratic system of government.

Taiwan Marriage Equality Act (2019): first country in Asia to legalize same-sex marriage, with full spousal rights and adoption access extended since 2023; Equity (EQU) dimension scores reflect this structural inclusion.

topSignals.title: Ukraine 50.0 of 100: Russia's July 1-2 Kyiv Mass Strike Scored Against Russia — 27-Plus Killed; Ukraine Confirmed at 50.0
topSignals.whyItMatters: Russia launched a mass strike on Kyiv on July 1-2: 70-plus missiles, approximately 500 drones, 27-plus civilians killed. Ukraine sheltered 52,500 residents in metro stations. The strike is Russia's conduct — scored against Russia (0 of 100, the absolute floor). Ukraine's civilian-protection response is scored on Ukraine's own conduct, which confirms at 50.0 of 100.
topSignals.description: Ukraine holds at 50.0 of 100 in the Functional band. The July 1-2 Kyiv mass strike — 70-plus missiles and approximately 500 drones launched; 27-plus civilians killed in residential and commercial areas — is Russia's deliberate targeting of civilian infrastructure and city centers. Under the benchmark's attribution rule, harm inflicted by another state cannot generate a downgrade for the target state. Ukraine is scored on its own conduct: the sheltering of 52,500 residents in metro stations, coordinated civilian evacuation under fire, and continuity of emergency services. That response is competent civilian protection, consistent with the Functional band at 50.0 of 100. Russia's deliberate targeting is scored against Russia, which holds at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor, reinforced for the eleventh consecutive cycle. Ukraine is confirmed at 50.0 of 100; confidence: high.

evidence.claim: OHCHR Ukraine Monitoring Mission July 2026: total documented civilian casualties in Ukraine reach 62,716 as of July 1; the 2026 casualty rate is running above the 2025 rate; continued deliberate targeting of residential areas, city centers, and energy infrastructure documented — all attributed to Russian forces.

Ukraine State Emergency Service July 2, 2026: 70-plus missiles and approximately 500 drones launched at Kyiv on July 1-2; 27-plus civilians confirmed dead; 52,500 residents sheltered in metro stations during the strike window.

topSignals.title: Venezuela 18.0 of 100: Day 8 Earthquake Response — Death Toll 2,595; July 8 Checkpoint Six Days Away
topSignals.whyItMatters: Venezuela's earthquake death toll rose to 2,595 on Day 8 of the June 24 response — up from 1,943 on Day 7. The July 8 date is six days away. That date is the standard 14-day assessment window for determining whether response failures reflect capacity or deliberate obstruction. Score holds at 18.0 of 100.
topSignals.description: Venezuela holds at 18.0 of 100. Day 8 of the response to the June 24 twin earthquakes (magnitude 7.2 and 7.5). The confirmed death toll rose to 2,595 — up 652 from 1,943 on Day 7. The sustained daily increment is consistent with ongoing search-and-rescue recovery operations. The July 8 date — the 14-day mark from the June 24 earthquake — is now six days out. The two-week mark is the standard assessment window for distinguishing government capacity failure from deliberate obstruction or diversion. Aid acceptance from international partners continues: prior cycles documented UNICEF 48-tonne supply shipments and a United States commitment of 300 million dollars under OFAC General License 60. Venezuela's own domestic relief delivery remains slow and access-restricted. The downgrade trigger — documented deliberate aid obstruction or diversion, or suppression of casualty data — has not materialized. The upgrade trigger — demonstrably effective, transparent, and sustained reconstruction — has not materialized. Both directions remain blocked; the net is sub-threshold. Venezuela is confirmed at 18.0 of 100; confidence: medium. Watch flags: Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) remain open through July 8.

evidence.claim: OCHA Venezuela July 2, 2026: confirmed death toll rose to 2,595 on Day 8 of the earthquake response, up from 1,943 on Day 7; domestic relief distribution remains slow and access-restricted; July 8 is the 14-day assessment checkpoint.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 of 100 and Burkina Faso 6.3 of 100: Near-Floor Confirmed; Ebola July Peak Window; Sahel Lean Season; Fifth First Baseline (Georgia 34.4) and Princeton University 57.8 Confirmed
topSignals.whyItMatters: The Democratic Republic of the Congo holds at 2.3 of 100 through cycle 19 of the Ebola emergency, with 1,333-plus cases and 399-plus deaths. Burkina Faso holds at 6.3 of 100. Georgia and Princeton University both received first formal baseline confirmations. Five first baselines were established in this cycle — a series record.
topSignals.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) holds at 2.3 of 100 — the 19th consecutive emergency-cycle assessment. The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak stands at 1,333-plus confirmed cases and 399-plus deaths, updated in the July 2 assessment window. France recorded the first EU-imported case on June 24. The July natural seasonal Ebola peak window is active. Contact tracing runs at approximately 45 percent. No approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain. The floor-crossing trigger — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors — has not occurred. DRC is confirmed at 2.3 of 100; confidence: high. Burkina Faso holds at 6.3 of 100. The Sahel lean-season crisis, 2.1 million internally displaced persons, 3.5 million people besieged, and the April 2026 dissolution of 118 NGOs continue the already-priced June 1 active-state-perpetration reweight. JNIM siege harm is non-state actor conduct — not scored against Burkina Faso. The Sahel calibration flag (Mali 12.5 of 100 vs. Burkina Faso 6.3 of 100) is in its ninth consecutive day. No coordinator review timeline is set. Georgia receives its first formal baseline at 34.4 of 100 in the Developing band, rank 106. The foreign-agents law and 580-day protest crackdown are chronic and already priced. The only new within-window datum is the release of two activists on July 1 — a mild positive, sub-threshold for an upgrade. Composite reconstructs to 34.4. Princeton University receives its first formal baseline at 57.8 of 100 in the Functional band, rank 5 among universities. The approximately 210 million dollar federal grant freeze is a government action targeting Princeton — not a Princeton harm event. Princeton's academic-freedom defense, no-loan financial-aid policy, and slavery-legacy acknowledgment work are consistent with 57.8. Seven weak dimensions zero the integration premium.

evidence.claim: WHO July 2026: Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in DRC at 1,333-plus confirmed cases and 399-plus deaths in the July 2 assessment window; four provinces affected in northeast DRC; PHEIC active since May 16; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; contact tracing at approximately 45 percent.

FAO West Africa and Sahel July 2026: nearly 52.8 million people face acute food insecurity during the 2026 lean season (June-August); Burkina Faso accounts for a significant share; 3.5 million people besieged; 2.1 million internally displaced.

boundaryWatch.trigger: If the proposal is applied, the score moves to 15.0 of 100 — Critical band. The forward upgrade trigger: due-process restoration for detained persons, reinstatement of civil-society access, and reversal of the indefinite re-election amendment together constitute the scored positive-conduct threshold. A new mass-detention escalation or further human-rights-defender imprisonment would be the scored deeper-downgrade event.
boundaryWatch.note: El Salvador at 20.3 of 100 sits 0.3 points above the Developing-to-Critical boundary at 20.0. Tonight's first formal assessment proposes 15.0 of 100 — a Developing-to-Critical crossing with a delta of -5.3. The published score remains 20.3 while the proposal is under review.

boundaryWatch.trigger: First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member — is the next scored threshold event. Law entered force July 1; enforcement has not been documented on July 1 or 2. A durable positive reversal in Hong Kong civil-society access would be the upgrade trigger toward 20.0.
boundaryWatch.note: China at 19.5 of 100 Critical sits 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1 — assimilation codified, Article 63 diaspora liability active. Second boundary-proximity watch cycle. No enforcement documented yet.

boundaryWatch.trigger: EU AI Act full applicability on August 2 — 31 days away. White House 30-day vetting framework for Fable 5 is the next forward factor. The model ban/clearance/lift arc resolved net-neutral June 30.
boundaryWatch.note: Anthropic sits 0.9 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. Twelfth consecutive cycle in this range. The Commerce Department export-control arc on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 resolved net-neutral June 30. EU AI Act applies August 2. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: NLRB review of the unfair-labor-practice charge filed after Apple closed its Towson, Maryland store — its first unionized store. An NLRB complaint or ruling is the conversion trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Apple sits 0.6 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. The Congressional Labor Caucus call for an NLRB investigation remains pre-adjudication. Eighth boundary-watch cycle. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A new ceasefire agreement AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanese territory AND reconstruction delivery together constitute scored positive own-conduct — the upgrade trigger from 17.2 toward 20.0. The June 28 Washington framework is closed; a new diplomatic process is required.
boundaryWatch.note: Lebanon at 17.2 of 100 Critical sits 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Israeli strikes continue; no new ceasefire framework as of July 2. Harm from Israeli strikes is attributed to Israel, not Lebanon. Second boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate obstruction of monsoon-flood relief is the downgrade trigger; equitable relief scale-up with durable Afghan-deportation reversal is the upgrade trigger toward 20.0. 2025 monsoon floods (6.9 million affected, 1,000-plus dead) are exogenous; scored on own response conduct.
boundaryWatch.note: Pakistan at 17.2 of 100 Critical sits 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. First formal baseline established tonight. Sidecar reconstructs 16.9 — within fractional-baseline tolerance. Afghan deportations and speech law already priced. First boundary-proximity watch entry.

boundaryWatch.trigger: July 8 checkpoint — documented aid obstruction or deliberate diversion is the downgrade trigger; sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger toward 20.0. Action and Accountability watch flags open.
boundaryWatch.note: Venezuela at 18.0 of 100 Critical sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Day 8 earthquake response; death toll 2,595; July 8 checkpoint six days out. Third boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Realized WFP food-assistance cuts plus a formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration in Borno with documented aid obstruction — August IPC lean-season update is the next checkpoint.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 18.0 of 100 Critical sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno priced in the June 19 band crossing. WFP imminent-cuts warning is a forward trigger. Second boundary-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window — active. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu is the floor-crossing trigger. France-imported case June 24 is the active EU-country imported chain. International spread does not affect trigger conditions.
boundaryWatch.note: DRC at 2.3 of 100 Critical through cycle 19 of the Ebola emergency. 1,333-plus cases and 399-plus deaths as of July 2; July natural peak window active. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. Floor-crossing trigger has not occurred.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A distinct civilian mass-casualty event from Hormuz navigation — not yet in evidence — would be a new scored threshold event toward the floor. Documented de-escalation or release of detained protesters at scale would be the upgrade trigger. Iran-US Doha talks are in day 2 as of July 2.
boundaryWatch.note: Iran at 2.5 of 100 Critical sits 2.5 points above the floor. 784-plus executions year-to-date — a 37-year high. Strait of Hormuz central channel mined; Gulf strikes continue an already-priced arc. Doha talks underway. Not assessed tonight; carry-forward from July 1. Second floor-proximity watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bengali Muslim and Rohingya expulsions documented in June 2026; detect-delete-deport digital surveillance continuing. Not assessed tonight.
boundaryWatch.note: India at 15.6 of 100 Critical sits 0.6 points above the Critical floor through an eighteenth consecutive monitoring cycle. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration covering Buur Hakaba or Baidoa combined with documented government aid obstruction is the scored change trigger. Famine risk window extends through September 2026.
boundaryWatch.note: Somalia at 4.7 of 100 Critical. Buur Hakaba at IPC AMN Phase 5 with GAM rate 37.1 percent. Not assessed tonight. Ninth floor-proximity cycle. Famine declaration window remains open through September 2026.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to stateless bidoon or migrant workers during wartime — Equity downgrade trigger. Durable verified expansion of bidoon rights or citizenship recognition — upgrade trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Kuwait at 25.0 of 100 Developing sits 5.0 points above the Critical boundary. EQU watch flag open on bidoon and migrant-worker emergency-protection performance under active war conditions. Fourth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review. Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) was reassessed in June on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. Mali's starting-point score of 12.5 is above the likely calibrated range. No timeline set.
boundaryWatch.note: Mali at 12.5 of 100 Critical on a starting-point seed score. The Sahel calibration flag is in its ninth consecutive day. The gap with Burkina Faso (6.3, reweighted June 1 on active-state-perpetration conduct) persists. Coordinator review covers Mali, Niger, and potentially Chad. Eighth boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DOJ False Claims Act complaint (kickbacks and disability discrimination) and Barrows v. Humana class action (nH Predict AI claim denials). Either concluded finding confirms the Developing-band designation.
boundaryWatch.note: Humana at 35.2 of 100 Developing, 4.8 points below the Functional boundary. Eleventh cycle post-downgrade. Not assessed tonight. Neither the DOJ complaint nor the Barrows class action has a fixed hearing date.

emergingRisks.risk: El Salvador's indefinite re-election amendment takes effect in 2027. The 2027 election will be the first governed by the July 2025 constitutional change. That event is the next structured trigger for the conduct pattern documented tonight.
emergingRisks.detail: The proposed score of 15.0 of 100 for El Salvador documents a Boundaries (BND) and Accountability (ACC) collapse driven by the indefinite re-election amendment, foreign-agents law, Cristosal exile, and mass detention. A 2027 election conducted under the new constitutional rules, further HRD imprisonment, or mass-detention escalation would add scored downward pressure. Genuine due-process restoration for detained persons and civil-society reinstatement are the upgrade triggers.

emergingRisks.risk: Russia's July 1-2 Kyiv mass strike adds to a 2026 civilian-casualty rate already running above 2025 levels. The July 1 OHCHR total of 62,716 documented casualties does not yet include the July 1-2 event. Ukraine continues its civilian-protection posture at 50.0 of 100.
emergingRisks.detail: Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor — continues deliberate targeting of civilian infrastructure and city centers. The July 1-2 strike is the largest single-day casualty event in the July assessment window. Ukraine at 50.0 of 100 Functional continues competent civilian protection. The scored downgrade trigger for Ukraine — documented Ukrainian-state failure to protect or discriminatory relief — has not occurred. The forward trigger for Russia is continuation of an already-priced pattern; no lower score exists.

emergingRisks.risk: Venezuela's earthquake death toll rose to 2,595 on Day 8 — a sustained daily increment. The July 8 assessment checkpoint is six days away. Domestic access remains restricted.
emergingRisks.detail: The two-week mark (July 8) is the standard window for distinguishing government capacity failure from deliberate aid obstruction or diversion. Documented deliberate obstruction or diversion is the Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. A demonstrably effective, transparent, and sustained reconstruction effort is the upgrade trigger. Venezuela is 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Ebola July natural peak window is active. The outbreak stands at 1,333-plus cases and 399-plus deaths. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. France-imported case June 24 is the active EU-country imported chain.
emergingRisks.detail: The July natural seasonal peak for Bundibugyo Ebola increases the risk that the outbreak outpaces response capacity. The floor-crossing trigger for DRC — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu — has not occurred. DRC borders South Sudan in Haut-Uele. A realized Ebola incursion into South Sudan combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response would constitute scorable own-conduct — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.

emergingRisks.risk: China's Article 63 enforcement forward trigger is now in its third day without a documented action. The first prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member tied explicitly to Article 63 remains the scored threshold event.
emergingRisks.detail: China at 19.5 of 100 sits 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1. Amnesty International and HRW both characterized the law as codifying existing practices — confirmed at 19.5 with the forward trigger logged. Governments with large Chinese diaspora populations — including Australia, Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United States — are the observable proxies for first Article 63 enforcement.

emergingRisks.risk: UnitedHealth Group's July 29 Q2 earnings call is the next material information event. DOJ criminal and civil probes into Optum Rx physician reimbursement and MA-inflation billing remain pre-adjudication.
emergingRisks.detail: Any material DOJ disclosure, investor-facing admission, indictment, or settlement at or around the July 29 earnings call would be scored under Accountability (ACC) and Integrity (INT) dimensions. The near-floor score of 10.2 already prices the Senate finding and prior probes. A concluded adverse finding constitutes a new scored event.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's RSF El Obeid offensive continues. Amnesty International documented El Fasher crimes-against-humanity conduct in the current assessment window. Sudan (0 of 100) was not in tonight's priority cluster; assessment carries forward from June 30.
emergingRisks.detail: Sudan at 0 of 100 on the absolute floor. The El Obeid RSF drone siege and El Fasher crimes-against-humanity documentation reinforce an already-priced floor. No lower score is possible. An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access would be scored positive own-conduct. Al-Burhan's ceasefire rejection stands as of the last assessment.

emergingRisks.risk: The Sahel calibration flag — Mali (12.5 of 100) vs. Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) — is in its ninth consecutive day without a coordinator review timeline.
emergingRisks.detail: Both confirmed at live baselines on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. The calibration review covers Mali, Niger, and potentially Chad. The gap has persisted through the entire June-July series. The JNIM siege harm attributed to non-state actors does not resolve the calibration question — the gap exists at the state-conduct level.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act becomes fully applicable on August 2 — 31 days from July 2. General-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. xAI and OpenAI have not published compliance roadmaps.
emergingRisks.detail: A post-applicability non-compliance finding for general-purpose AI models constitutes a scored event. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented at 59.1 Functional. OpenAI and xAI compliance gaps will become legally significant on August 2. The EU AI Act is the next sector-wide scored deadline for the AI Labs Index.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — El Salvador Band-Crossing Proposal (First Baseline, Codified Conduct)
sectorAlerts.headline: El Salvador's first formal assessment proposes a Developing-to-Critical band crossing at 15.0 of 100 — codified institutional harm across seven dimensions, with the Action dimension held at 2.4 for homicide reduction.
observations: The 20.3 seed score was never assessed with per-entity evidence. Tonight's formal baseline applies the canonical composite formula to dated, documented in-force conduct: the July 2025 indefinite re-election amendment, the May 2025 foreign-agents law, the forced exile of Cristosal, the arrest of anti-corruption chief Ruth Lopez, 140-plus human rights defenders exiled, and a three-year state of exception with approximately 87,000 detained without due process.

The Boundaries (BND) dimension shows the largest single-dimension delta at -0.6 from the published seed (2.0 to proposed 1.4). The indefinite re-election amendment and foreign-agents law both score against the Boundaries dimension, which assesses whether an institution respects procedural and constitutional limits on power.

The Action (ACT) dimension is held at 2.4 — above the published seed of 2.0 — to credit genuine homicide reduction. El Salvador's homicide rate declined from one of the world's highest to among its lowest under the current administration. This credit is analytically distinct from the detention mechanism and reflects the scored public-safety outcome.

The proposed 15.0 of 100 places El Salvador above Bahrain (9.4 of 100) and Mali (12.5 of 100), reflecting that the ACT-dimension homicide-reduction credit genuinely distinguishes the case from state-perpetration-dominant scorers. The conservative anchoring at 15.0 rather than below 10 is the load-bearing analytical decision in this assessment.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — First Baseline Cohort (Taiwan 83.0, Pakistan 17.2, Georgia 34.4, Princeton 57.8)
sectorAlerts.headline: Five first baselines established in one cycle — the largest single-cycle cohort in the June-July series; Taiwan Exemplary at 83.0 is the highest-scoring country assessed this cycle.
observations: Taiwan (83.0 Exemplary, rank 9): base 75.0 plus integration premium 8.0. One weak dimension (Equity at 3.5) slightly lowers the maximum premium. Universal healthcare, first-in-Asia marriage equality, full democratic governance, and competent civilian protection from Chinese military pressure all support the Exemplary band. Chinese coercion is attributed to China and scored against China only.

Pakistan (17.2 Critical, rank ~140): first full baseline. Sidecar reconstructs 16.9 — within 0.3 fractional-baseline tolerance of the published 17.2. The 2025 monsoon floods are exogenous; Afghan deportations and the speech-restriction law are already priced in the May 2025 downgrade. The 2.8-point distance from the Critical-to-Developing boundary makes this a new boundary-proximity entry.

Georgia (34.4 Developing, rank 106): composite reconstructs exactly to 34.4. The foreign-agents law and 580-day protest crackdown are chronic and priced; two activists released July 1 is a mild positive but sub-threshold. Georgia's Developing-band score reflects a structural backsliding pattern that prevents Functional-band access.

Princeton University (57.8 Functional, rank 5 universities): first formal baseline. The approximately 210 million dollar federal grant freeze is a government action on Princeton — not a Princeton harm event and not a downgrade basis. Academic-freedom defense, no-loan financial-aid policy, and slavery-legacy work are consistent with Functional. Seven weak dimensions zero the integration premium. Sidecar reconstructs 58.1.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Ukraine and Russia Attribution (July 1-2 Kyiv Strike)
sectorAlerts.headline: Russia's July 1-2 Kyiv mass strike — 27-plus killed, 70-plus missiles, approximately 500 drones — is scored against Russia (0 of 100); Ukraine's civilian-protection response confirms the Functional band at 50.0 of 100.
observations: The benchmark's attribution rule (§3e-bis rule 3) holds that harm inflicted by one state on another cannot generate a downgrade for the target state. Russia's deliberate targeting of city centers and energy infrastructure is unambiguously Russia's conduct. Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor — carries no lower score.

Ukraine at 50.0 of 100 Functional is scored on its own conduct: the sheltering of 52,500 Kyiv residents in metro stations during the July 1-2 strike window, coordinated civilian evacuation under fire, and continuity of emergency services. This is competent civilian protection consistent with the Functional band.

The 2026 Russia civilian-casualty rate is already running above the 2025 rate, per the OHCHR July 1 report documenting 62,716 total casualties. The July 1-2 event adds to this count in the next OHCHR reporting cycle.

This is the 11th consecutive cycle applying the same §3e-bis(3) attribution rule without exception to the Russia-Ukraine conflict. The rule's consistent application is methodologically significant: it separates the harm event from the scored entity in all cases, not only when the attribution is politically favorable.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Critical Cluster and Venezuela Earthquake (Day 8)
sectorAlerts.headline: Ten of 15 assessed entities hold in the Critical band; Venezuela reaches 2,595 dead on Day 8 with the July 8 checkpoint six days out; DRC Ebola enters July peak window.
observations: Ten of tonight's 15 assessed entities score in the Critical band (below 20.0 of 100): DRC (2.3), Burkina Faso (6.3), Bahrain (9.4), UnitedHealth Group (10.2), Mali (12.5), Pakistan (17.2), Lebanon (17.2), Nigeria (18.0), Venezuela (18.0), China (19.5). El Salvador at 20.3 published (proposed 15.0) would be an eleventh if the proposal applies.

Venezuela's Day 8 death toll of 2,595 (up from 1,943 on Day 7) represents a sustained 600-plus daily increment consistent with ongoing search-and-rescue recovery. The July 8 two-week checkpoint is the standard window for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction. Both the Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags remain open.

DRC Ebola enters the July natural seasonal peak window at 1,333-plus cases and 399-plus deaths (updated in the July 2 assessment window). The France-imported case from June 24 is the active EU-country import chain. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. The floor-crossing trigger has not occurred.

Sudan (0 of 100), South Sudan (0 of 100), Somalia (4.7), Yemen (0 of 100), Iran (2.5), and Russia (0 of 100) are not in tonight's priority cluster. They carry forward at their last-assessed scores. Iran-US Doha talks are in day 2 as of July 2; no scored outcome.

sectorAlerts.sector: Methodology — Subdimension Sidecar Milestone; First-Baseline Procedure
sectorAlerts.headline: Five structured subdimension evidence records written tonight — the first cycle to produce per-subdimension evidence at scale; the first-baseline procedure applied to five entities simultaneously.
observations: Subdimension sidecars carry per-subdimension evidence items linked to specific codes (A1-A5 for Awareness, E1-E5 for Empathy, etc.). Tonight's five sidecars — el-salvador (40 subdimension evidence items), pakistan, taiwan, princeton-university, georgia — are the first time this layer has been produced at scale. Prior cycles had no structured subdimension evidence.

The first-baseline procedure distinguishes seed scores (bulk-import estimates) from evidence-based composites. All five first baselines tonight follow the same procedure: apply the canonical formula to dated, documented within-window conduct. El Salvador is the only one where the formula produces a score that differs from the seed by more than the 5-point change threshold.

The subdimension sidecar data enables future dimension-level trend analysis: tracking, for example, whether the Boundaries (BND) dimension is systematically declining across a sector, or whether the Action (ACT) dimension shows a countertrend in certain government types. This analysis was not previously possible from dimensional-summary data alone.

The false-positive screening framework applied 13 screens tonight across four rule categories: attribution/directionality, exogenous events, pre-adjudication, and double-count/non-state. The 13-screen count matches June 30 as the second-highest in the series (July 1 set the record at 14). The consistency of the screening results — all 13 resolved to confirmation under the same framework — validates the rules' operational stability.

methodologyNotes.name: First-Baseline Codified-Conduct Assessment — El Salvador
methodologyNotes.description: El Salvador's first formal assessment establishes the first-baseline codified-conduct procedure. The seed score of 20.3 was a bulk-import estimate, not an evidence-based composite. The formal assessment applies the canonical formula to in-force own-conduct dated within the scoring window. Three classes of evidence drive the assessment: (1) codified legal changes — the July 2025 indefinite re-election amendment (in force) and the May 2025 foreign-agents law (in force); (2) state actions against civil society — Cristosal forced into exile, anti-corruption chief arrested, 140-plus human rights defenders exiled; (3) ongoing institutional harm — three-year state of exception with approximately 87,000 detained without due process and custodial deaths documented. The Action (ACT) dimension is held at 2.4 to credit genuine homicide reduction — a deliberate trade-off that scores the public-safety outcome separately from the detention mechanism. The composite of 15.0 is conservative relative to state-perpetration-dominant peers. Medium confidence is assigned because no prior baseline exists for drift detection; confidence will upgrade to high after the first reassessment.

methodologyNotes.name: Subdimension Sidecar Records — First Cycle at Scale
methodologyNotes.description: Five structured subdimension sidecar records were written tonight — the first time the pipeline has produced per-subdimension evidence at scale. Each sidecar links evidence items to specific subdimension codes (40 subdimensions across 8 dimensions). Sources carry tier ratings (Tier 1-5), URLs, and dated quotes. This data layer enables dimension-trend analysis and cross-entity subdimension comparison that were not previously possible. The sidecar format is a v1.3 methodology candidate feature entering the evidence-accrual phase.

methodologyNotes.name: False-Positive Attribution Framework — 13 Screens Applied
methodologyNotes.description: Thirteen false-positive screens were applied tonight across four rule categories. Attribution/directionality (§3e-bis rule 3): Ukraine (Kyiv attack by Russia), Taiwan (coercion by China), Bahrain (Iranian drone strikes), Lebanon (Israeli strikes), DRC (Ebola epidemiological progression, not government conduct). Exogenous events: Pakistan (monsoon floods are natural disaster), Venezuela (earthquake is exogenous), Princeton University (federal grant freeze is a government action on Princeton). Pre-adjudication (§3e-bis rule 1): UnitedHealth Group (DOJ probes are investigations, not adjudicated findings). Double-count and threshold (§3e-bis rule 4): China (Ethnic Unity Law codifies already-priced practice; Article 63 has no documented enforcement), Nigeria (IPC Phase 5 already priced in the June 19 crossing). Non-state actor and calibration (§3e-bis rules 3 and 5): Mali and Burkina Faso (JNIM siege harm is non-state; Sahel calibration routed to coordinator level). Georgia (§3e-bis rule 4 double-count — chronic evidence already priced).

forwardTriggers.trigger: 14-day reassessment checkpoint — standard interval for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction. Documented aid obstruction, diversion, or suppression of casualty data is the downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. Sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger. Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags open. Death toll 2,595; Day 8.
Full resolution-phase reassessment due. Conversion trigger: reversal of the June 8 codified-impunity law and release of detained union leaders.

Q2 2026 earnings call — material DOJ disclosures, investor-facing admissions about probe scope, or settlement announcements are scored triggers under Accountability (ACC) and Integrity (INT) dimensions. DOJ probes cover Optum Rx physician reimbursement and MA-inflation billing.

July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window active. 1,333-plus cases and 399-plus deaths as of July 2. France-imported case June 24 is the active EU-country import chain. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors is the floor-crossing trigger toward 0 of 100.
EU AI Act full applicability — general-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. No public compliance roadmap from xAI as of this cycle.
EU AI Act full applicability — same obligations apply. 42-state attorney general proceedings ongoing. No confirmed compliance roadmap.
EU AI Act full applicability — 31 days away. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented. White House 30-day vetting framework resolution is the next SYS and Boundaries forward factor.

August IPC lean-season update — IPC Phase 5 Borno trajectory and government response adequacy. Actual WFP food-assistance cuts and a realized Phase 5 famine declaration would be scored triggers from the June 19 band crossing to 18.0 of 100.

Famine declaration window open through September 2026. Buur Hakaba and Baidoa both at IPC AMN Phase 5. A formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration in Somalia combined with documented government aid obstruction is a scored change trigger.

2027 election under the July 2025 indefinite re-election constitutional amendment is the next structured forward trigger. A further HRD imprisonment or mass-detention escalation constitutes a scored deeper-downgrade event. Due-process restoration and civil-society reinstatement are the upgrade triggers from the proposed Critical band.

First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member. Law entered force July 1; enforcement not documented on July 1 or 2. Any first enforcement constitutes new realized harm scorable toward Equity and Boundaries docks.

Russia at 0 of 100 — the absolute floor. Continuation of already-priced deliberate targeting of city centers and energy infrastructure. No lower score is possible. The July 1-2 Kyiv mass strike (27-plus killed) is the most recent scored event.

An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access is scored positive own-conduct. Amnesty International documented El Fasher crimes-against-humanity conduct in the current window. RSF El Obeid offensive continuing.

A new ceasefire agreement AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanon AND reconstruction delivery are the scored positive-conduct triggers from 17.2 of 100. The June 28 Washington framework is closed.

Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to stateless bidoon or migrant workers during wartime — Equity (EQU) downgrade trigger from 25.0 of 100. Durable and independently verified expansion of bidoon rights or citizenship recognition — upgrade trigger.

A realized Ebola incursion from DRC's Haut-Uele province into South Sudan, combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response, would constitute scorable own-conduct — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series.

Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review — Mali (12.5 of 100) versus reweighted peer Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. Calibration flag in ninth consecutive day; no timeline set.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: El Salvador holds the Action dimension at 2.4 on documented homicide reduction while six other dimensions propose Critical-band scores. If the mass-detention state of exception ends and detainees are released, would the July 2025 indefinite-re-election amendment and Cristosal exile alone hold the Boundaries and Accountability dimensions at Critical-band levels?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: ACT credits the public-safety outcome; BND, ACC, and INT penalize the mechanism — can the credit and the penalty coexist independently?
